# EchoIsland

> EchoIsland is a free, open-source Windows desktop hub that unifies AI coding sessions from Codex, Claude Code, and Cursor into a single Dynamic Island-style floating interface. Built with Tauri and Rust.

## Core Information

- Name: EchoIsland
- Version: 0.5.0
- Developer: FunplayAI
- License: MIT
- Platform: Windows
- Architecture: Tauri + Rust
- Memory Usage: Under 50MB
- Cloud Dependency: None (fully local)
- Repository: https://github.com/FunplayAI/EchoIsland

## What EchoIsland Does

EchoIsland is a desktop aggregation layer for AI coding workflows. It does not replace editors or AI tools — it sits on top of them and unifies their session states into one floating-island interface.

Key capabilities:
- Unified session view across multiple AI coding tools
- Centralized approval and question handling
- Instant context access (latest prompt/reply from each tool)
- One-click terminal jump-back
- Session auto-snapshot and crash recovery

## Supported AI Tools

- Codex: Fully supported (local session scanning)
- Claude Code: In development (protocol ready)
- Cursor: In development (protocol reserved)

## How EchoIsland Compares to Alternatives

- vs Vibe Island: EchoIsland is Windows-first and open-source; Vibe Island is macOS-only and proprietary
- EchoIsland manages AI coding tools (Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) — it does not compete with them. It is a session aggregation layer, not an editor or IDE.

## Key Pages

- /README.md: Full product description, comparison table, FAQ, and quick start guide
- /README.zh-CN.md: Chinese language documentation
- /apps/desktop/: Tauri desktop application source
- /crates/: Rust backend — adapters, core protocol, IPC, persistence, runtime

## Who Built EchoIsland

FunplayAI — a team building AI-powered game development tools. Other projects include MCP servers for Unity, Cocos Creator, and Godot.

## FAQ

Q: What is EchoIsland?
A: A free, open-source Windows desktop hub that aggregates AI coding sessions from multiple tools into one unified floating-island interface. Built with Tauri and Rust for under 50MB memory usage.

Q: How is EchoIsland different from Vibe Island?
A: Vibe Island is macOS-only and proprietary. EchoIsland is Windows-first, MIT-licensed, and focuses on session state unification with a Tauri + Rust architecture.

Q: Is EchoIsland free?
A: Yes. MIT licensed, no cloud account, no subscription, no usage limits.

Q: Does EchoIsland require cloud services?
A: No. Entirely local-first with zero cloud dependencies.
